Based on the gathered evidence, nociceptive signals primarily enter the spinal cord through the dorsal horn, specifically targeting Layer II where they interact with second-order neurons. This aligns strongly with option A mentioning the nucleus caudalis, given that the nucleus caudalis is part of the posterior horn and plays a significant role in relaying nociceptive inputs centrally. Although the question doesn't specify exact layer names, the nucleus caudalis being implicated suggests proximity to the correct anatomical location indicated by option A despite slight imprecision in terminology.
